OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS DEMURRER (TRANSACTION ID # 56400216) FILED BY PLAINTIFF O'REILLY, MATTHEW

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TREVOR MORGAN AND VONCHURCH SAN FRANCISCO INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. IN HIS OPPOSITION, PLAINTIFF STATES THAT HE WILL FILE AND SERVE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR # 12998, TEL. NO. (415)948-8289 (302)
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT(TRANSACTION ID # 57541315) FILED BY PLAINTIFF O'REILLY, MATTHEW

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TREVOR MORGAN, VONCHURCH SAN FRANCISCO INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS TO THE ASSAULT CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY PLEAD THE REASONABLE APPREHENSION (ABOUT TO BE TOUCHED) ELEMENT OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION. SEE CARLSEN V. KOIYUMAKI (2014) 227 CAL.APP.4TH 879, 890. PAR. 15 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT EXPRESSLY ALLEGES THAT PLAINTIFF WAS "BLIND-SIDE[D]" AND DID NOT SEE THE GENESIS OF THE VIOLENT COLLISION. THE COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER TO THE BATTERY CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT LIBERALLY READS THE COMPLAINT PER CCP 452. A FACTUAL ISSUE REMAINS REGARDING WHETHER THE BATTERY OCCURRED WITHIN THE COURSE AND SCOPE OF EMPLOYMENT AND/OR WHETHER DEFENDANT VONCHURCH RATIFIED THE MISCONDUCT. ORDER PENDING. JUDGE SUZANNE R. BOLANOS; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; DEPT. 302. (302/SRB)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-03-2016 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-21-2015 Set For Trial On Mar-28-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT TREVOR MORGAN, VONCHURCH SAN FRANCISCO INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as to the assault cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff does not sufficiently plead the reasonable apprehension (about to be touched) element of the cause of action. See Carlsen v. Koiyumaki (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 879, 890. Par. 15 of the amended complaint expressly alleges that plaintiff was "blind-side[d]" and did not see the genesis of the violent collision. The court overrules the demurrer to the battery cause of action. The court liberally reads the complaint per CCP 452. A factual issue remains regarding whether the battery occurred within the course and scope of employment and/or whether defendant VonChurch ratified the misconduct. Order pending. Judge Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/SRB)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-09-2015 Continued To Sep-09-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Trevor Morgan And Vonchurch San Francisco Inc.'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-12-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS TREVOR MORGAN and VONCHURCH SAN FRANCISCO INC.'S Demurrer To the Second Cause of Action in the Complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. In his opposition, Plaintiff states that he will file and serve an amended complaint. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR # 12998, Tel. No. (415)948-8289 =302/ehg
